title: The Future Is Domain-Specific Agents - Justin Schroeder, StandardAgents
summary: Justin Schroeder argues that domain-specific agents composed together form a superior architecture compared to monolithic skills. He contends that this composition-based approach enables smaller models to perform real work, improves reliability and cost efficiency, and moves AI from internal tools to customer-facing products.
writeup: Justin Schroeder of StandardAgents makes the case that composing narrow, domain-specific agents beats building monolithic, all-purpose skills. The talk's core claim: composition lets smaller models do real work while improving reliability and cost efficiency. The argued payoff is moving AI past internal tooling and into customer-facing products. Supporting concepts include agent orchestration and test-time compute.
